name: Nheengatu
notes: Nheengatu (nheẽgatú, 'Modern Tupí' / Amazonian General Language), a Tupí-Guaraní (Tupian) language of the upper Rio Negro, Brazil, Colombia and Venezuela. The modern orthography is broadly phonemic: five oral vowels ⟨a e i o u⟩ with nasal counterparts ⟨ã ẽ ĩ õ ũ⟩, and the acute accent ⟨á é í ó ú⟩ marks stress on the plain vowel quality. Consonants map one-to-one: ⟨p t k b d g m n s⟩ as written, ⟨r⟩ = /r/ (tapped [ɾ] intervocalically), ⟨w⟩ = /w/, ⟨x⟩ = /ʃ/, ⟨j⟩ = /ʒ/, ⟨y⟩ = the palatal glide /j/, ⟨h⟩ = /h/, and the digraph ⟨nh⟩ = /ɲ/. Regressive vowel nasalisation before a nasal consonant (⟨nheenga⟩ → [ɲeẽɡa]) is a phonetic process not marked in the spelling and is not resolved here.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors

sources.notes: Vowel system (oral/nasal), consonant inventory including /ʃ/ ⟨x⟩, /ʒ/ ⟨j⟩, /ɲ/ ⟨nh⟩ and the palatal glide ⟨y⟩, and the acute stress accent.
